title: Is Evangelion Exhibition worth an 8 hour drive
display_title: Is Evangelion Exhibition worth an 8 hour drive
wikitext:
[]: Is Evangelion Exhibition worth an 8 hour drive?             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Thu Jan 25, 2018 11:41 am I nearly lost my mind last night when I found out that not only was Eva Exhibition coming to Houston, only 8 hours away from me, but that it was the first time it had ever been outside of Japan. I know I could just fly and get there in like an hour, but there's no way I can afford plane tickets. If I do go, it will be my first solo road trip since getting my license, my first convention ever, and the first time I would be staying somewhere by myself (there's no way I could drive there, attend, and drive back in one day). Do you think it's worth it?

[]: Re: Is Evangelion Exhibition worth an 8 hour drive?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 25, 2018 11:46 am AdamMalkobitch wrote: ...it was the first time it had ever been outside of Japan.

[]: Re: Is Evangelion Exhibition worth an 8 hour drive?             Quote Post by Hark » Thu Jan 25, 2018 1:00 pm sure , why not ?

[]: Re: Is Evangelion Exhibition worth an 8 hour drive?             Quote Post by AR-99 » Thu Jan 25, 2018 5:04 pm Think the OP meant to say that this was the first time this particular exhibit had been outside of Japan. It will be at Anime Matsuri: http://www.animematsuri.com/8779/evange ... e-matsuri/ I say do whatever it takes to go. Round up some friends to go to Anime Matsuri with you to split driving duties. Someone I know drove from New York down to Anime Weekend Atlanta last year - he arrived on Saturday, was going to drive back on Sunday afternoon to be at work on Monday ). If you wanted to see the exhibit otherwise, you would need to fly to Japan, so what's an 8 hour drive?

[]: Re: Is Evangelion Exhibition worth an 8 hour drive?             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jan 28, 2018 3:28 pm Last year Matsuri had a Sailor Moon event (and implied more to come this year, in addition to bringing over this year's Eva Exhibit) but I personally can't support it due to numerous shady dealings and disturbing stories from Lolita fashion friends that have attended. That said, if you think you'll have fun and most importantly stay safe, definitely enjoy yourself if you make the trip to H-town

[]: Re: Is Evangelion Exhibition worth an 8 hour drive?             Quote Post by tism » Thu Feb 01, 2018 12:23 am Well, I'm not driving but I am flying from norhern Canada to Houston. VIP and NERV pass purchased. Very excited about the exhibit and Eva store.

[]: Re: Is Evangelion Exhibition worth an 8 hour drive?             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sun Mar 18, 2018 8:17 pm Original 2/1 post: ^The Evastore went to Anime Expo in LA last year, but I've been to the Evastore in Ikebukuro 2x now and I can't justify paying the markup, so did not end up getting anything. That said, it's a chance to check out their wares in person. I thought about going to check out the exhibit, which would be the only solid draw for me at this point. Natsu Dagneel's seiyu is coming but otherwise I could care less about the con's guestlist. 3/18 update: Noticed the below in my IG feed. https://www.instagram.com/p/BftfKgCFolR ... imematsuri Surprised to see that the Eva-01 statue has been shipped over (unless there are multiple copies of it). I've seen this in person at the 2014 Tokyo Film Festival when I watched 2.0. Bonus, it was subbed. https://www.instagram.com/p/BgcmzXAnI9c ... imematsuri Damnit I want that lanyard!

I know I could just fly and get there in like an hour, but there's no way I can afford plane tickets. If I do go, it will be my
